item.title: Tony Awards winners list for 2026: Live updates
item.description: The 2026 Tony Awards are underway, honoring the best of Broadway's plays and musicals.

item.title: 12 hurt in "gunfight" at Ohio festival, police say
item.description: Twelve people were injured, two of them critically, in what police are calling a "gunfight" that broke out at the Old West End Festival in Toledo, Ohio, on Saturday. Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Trump says he told Israel not to retaliate for Iranian strike
item.description: President Trump told several outlets Sunday he is asking Israel not to retaliate after Iran's strikes, hopeful that he can somehow preserve this very fragile ceasefire. Olivia Rinaldi reports.

item.title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake shakes southern Philippines, triggers tsunami watch
item.description: Tsunami waves were possible in the Phillipines, Taiwan, Japan, Guam, Papua New Guinea and several island nations and territories in the western Pacific.

item.title: Iran retaliates after Israel struck Beirut, accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire
item.description: Israel said Sunday that Iran has launched missiles at it in the first such bombardment since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April.

item.title: Iran strikes Israel for first time since April ceasefire in retaliation for Israeli attack on Beirut
item.description: For the first time since the ceasefire went into effect in April, Iran launched missiles toward Israel Sunday, largely contained to the north of the country. The Iranian retaliation came after Israeli forces bombed suspected Hezbollah strongholds in southern Beirut. Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Raman passes Pratt in LA mayoral race; California governor matchup undecided
item.description: Los Angeles City Councilwoman Nithya Raman has surpassed political newcomer Spencer Pratt in the race for L.A. mayor after trailing by nearly 6% on election night.

item.title: Studying dogs may reveal path to longer, healthier life for dogs, humans | 60 Minutes
item.description: The Dog Aging Project is working to help dogs live longer, healthier lives. The research results may help humans age well, too.

item.title: One dog's impact on the science of aging
item.description: The Rowe family dog Ralph was one of many canines with dementia who participated in a study of rapamycin. Scientists were able to gain new insight into the drug's potential as a treatment by studying his brain.

item.title: Collapse of U.S. shipbuilding poses national and economic security risks | 60 Minutes
item.description: China rolls out over 1,000 cargo ships a year, while the U.S. – maybe three. The Trump administration has called this a crisis with both economic and national security risks.

item.title: Dog aging research may help canines, people live longer and healthier
item.description: The Dog Aging Project is working to help dogs live longer, healthier lives. The research results may help humans age well, too.

item.title: This federal judge ruled against Trump. Then the death threats rolled in | 60 Minutes
item.description: Federal judges say criticism from President Trump can put their safety at risk. The White House says the president "understands the dangers of political violence."

item.title: Trump says U.S. shipbuilding is in a crisis as it lags far behind China
item.description: China rolls out over 1,000 cargo ships a year, while the U.S. – maybe three. The Trump administration has called this a crisis with both economic and national security risks.

item.title: Federal judges face threats after ruling against the Trump administration
item.description: Federal judges say criticism from President Trump can put their safety at risk. The White House says the president "understands the dangers of political violence."

item.title: Lawsuit attempts to stop UFC fight at White House on Trump's birthday
item.description: The lawsuit calls the event "deeply corrupt" and argues that it seeks to enrich the president and his allies and lacks proper authorization.

item.title: Teen dies after showing symptoms of heat-related illness on Grand Canyon hike
item.description: An 18-year-old died last week on a hike deep in the Grand Canyon, after showing symptoms of heat-related illness, the National Park Service said.

item.title: Transcript: Rep. Don Bacon on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 7, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Rep. Don Bacon, Republican of Nebraska, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 7, 2026.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 7, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Reps. Ro Khanna and Don Bacon join Margaret Brennan.

item.title: Alexander Zverev becomes Grand Slam champion with French Open win
item.description: In his fourth major final, Alexander Zverev beat Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7 (5), 6-1 for the French Open title on Sunday.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Khanna condemns Platner's past actions, but "they didn't come as a surprise" to Maine voters
item.description: "His actions were misogynistic, they were shameful, they were wrong," Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna, who campaigned with the Senate candidate on Friday, said.

item.title: Face the Nation: Bacon, Krebs, Buchanan, Barcott
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? GOP Rep. Don Bacon of Nebrask, cybersecurity expert Chris Krebs and former Biden AI adviser Ben Buchanan discuss artificial intelligence, while Rye Barcott, the co-founder of With Honor and author of "Courage Can Save Us: Ten Extraordinary Americans and the Fight for Our Future," also joins.

item.title: How retailers are using dynamic pricing based on consumer behavior
item.description: CBS California Investigates reviewed online shopping carts at three major retailers selected randomly. We found prices fluctuated significantly over a period of weeks, making it difficult to determine when the best price is.

item.title: 6/7: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Reps. Ro Khanna, Jim Himes and Don Bacon join. Plus, a panel on artificial intelligence with Chris Krebs and Ben Buchanan.

item.title: Marine veteran who works to get GIs elected on finding "a round into public service"
item.description: Rye Barcott, a Marine veteran who co-founded With Honor, a group that works to elect service members, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that one of the key takeaways for his new book "Courage Can Save Us" is "to find a route into public service, and that's one of the goals with this."

item.title: Anthropic adviser says it's "not hypothetical" that AI could abet biological weapons risk
item.description: CBS News contributor Chris Krebs, who ran CISA in the first Trump administration, and Ben Buchanan, who advised President Biden and is now a professor at Johns Hopkins and an adviser to Anthropic, joined "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" to discuss whether the government should regulate AI -- and if so, how it should be regulated.

item.title: GOP Rep. Don Bacon says Trump's endorsement of Ken Paxton is Texas was a "mistake"
item.description: Republican Rep. Don Bacon of Nebraska told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that President Trump's decision to endorse Ken Paxton in the Texas runoff for the Senate seat was a "mistake," adding "I think this has hurt the president."

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 7)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Young applicants discuss the challenges of today's job search
item.description: With the unemployment rate for young workers about twice as high as the national average, "Sunday Morning" talks with recent graduates from across the country about how AI is affecting both their prospects and the hiring process itself.

item.title: Treasury Department plans to use Iranian assets to help U.S. Gulf allies recover
item.description: The Treasury Department will use Iranian assets to help U.S. Gulf allies recover from damage caused by Tehran's regime, a source familiar with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's thinking told CBS News.
